overrides.override_summary: Franchisor's (Hyatt Place Franchising, L.L.C.) unaudited balance sheet required as supplemental disclosure. Total assets $72.03M, member's equity $53.2M.
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides franchisor entity-level financial visibility separate from parent's consolidated statements

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of franchise law claims or disclaimer of reliance permitted. Supersedes any other document term.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to bring fraud-in-inducement and reliance claims regardless of agreement language

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/transfer will not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain right to bring claims under state franchise law even after signing a general release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law. Franchisor will enforce to extent enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy-based termination trigger may not be effective in Maryland

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland Franchise Law, subject to arbitration obligations unless preempted by FAA.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees need not litigate exclusively in Illinois for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois choice-of-law provision does not override Maryland franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides specific statute of limitations for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days' notice for non-renewal required under Minn. Stat. §80C.14.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer cure and notice periods than standard FA terms (24h-30 days). Provides Minnesota franchisees more time to cure defaults.

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/transfer will not apply to claims arising under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees retain state franchise law claims despite signing release

overrides.override_summary: Minn. Rule Part 2860.4400J prohibits waiver of jury trial, waiver of procedures/forums/remedies, and consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages and termination penalties may be unenforceable in Minnesota. Jury trial rights preserved.

overrides.override_summary: Litigation cannot be required outside Minnesota. Franchisee retains right to submit matters to jurisdiction of Minnesota courts, subject to arbitration obligation.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois forum selection clause does not apply to Minnesota franchisees for state law claims

overrides.override_summary: Commissioner has determined termination/liquidated damages to be unfair, unjust and inequitable under Section 51-19-09. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions may be unenforceable in North Dakota — significant protection for franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/transfer will not apply to claims arising under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees retain state franchise law claims despite signing release

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration location may need to be mutually agreed (not Chicago) to the extent required by North Dakota law, unless preempted by FAA.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may not be forced to arbitrate in Chicago

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to the extent required by law, replacing Illinois choice of law.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchise law protections apply instead of Illinois law

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; 30-day cure period. Refusal to renew requires fair market value compensation. Cannot require out-of-state arbitration/litigation.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan provides strongest franchisee protections including FMV compensation for non-renewal and prohibition on out-of-state dispute resolution.

riders_identified: Rider to Franchise Agreement for use in Maryland
Rider to Confidentiality Agreement for use in Maryland
Rider to Franchise Agreement for use in Minnesota
Rider to Confidentiality Agreement for use in Minnesota
Rider to Confidentiality Agreement for use in North Dakota

release_form.title: Grant of Franchisor Consent and Franchisee Release
release_form.scope: Broad general release of all claims from beginning of time
